article_items.title: Five labs, five minds: building a multi-model finance drama on small models
article_items.description: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face
article_items.excerpt: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face
article_items.summary: Thousand Token Wood: shipping a multi-agent economy on a 3B model A lean base image does not ship it, so all four models failed identically with "could not find nvcc" until I based them on a CUDA devel image. Standing four distinct models up on one platform surfaced the real lesson: the friction is almost entirely at the serving layer, not the modeling layer. And the biggest change is under the hood: every creature now thinks with a different lab's small model. You can whisper a tip to a creature that is true (a real forecast of the next market mania the deck will draw, your genuine edge) or false (bait). The thing that made four heterogeneous models tractable was the same primitive that made one model tractable in v1: a tolerant JSON parse-and-repair layer that every.

key_points: A Blog post by Build Small Hackathon on Hugging Face Five labs, five minds: building a multi-model finance drama on small models The first version of Thousand Token Wood was a weather-god sandbox: five woodland.
The thing that made four heterogeneous models tractable was the same primitive that made one model tractable in v1: a tolerant JSON parse-and-repair layer that every model's output flows through.
That test is the most important one in the suite.
A market is interesting when the participants genuinely differ, and four labs' models trained on different data with different post-training are about as different as small models get.
This is the engineering report.
Heterogeneity is the product, not a constraint The obvious way to run a council of agents is one model, many prompts.
Secret information given to an agent is a firewall problem, and the firewall belongs in the data flow, proven by a test, not in a prompt instruction.
The whole council is open, and so are the traces.
Standing four distinct models up on one platform surfaced the real lesson: the friction is almost entirely at the serving layer, not the modeling layer.
Takeaways for building with small models A small model is a reliable format generator and an unreliable reasoner; you close the gap with structure, prompting, and a small fine-tune, not with scale.
And the biggest change is under the hood: every creature now thinks with a different lab's small model.
The owl hoards differently than the fox speculates.

article_items.title: Burn-9 makes an entire game out of Metal Gear's Codec scenes - Engadget
article_items.description: Plus, check out the new trailer for the cosmic horror game Penguin Colony.
article_items.excerpt: Plus, check out the new trailer for the cosmic horror game Penguin Colony.
article_items.summary: Announced today during the Story-Rich Showcase, Burn-9 is described as a "reverse Metal Gear." As a radio operator, it's your job to guide a secret agent, who goes by the handle Dodo, as they try to salvage a mission that has gone horribly wrong. Separately, Fellow Traveller shared a new trailer for Penguin Colony , the next game from Origame Digital, the studio behind 2020's excellent Umurangi Generation . Your interactions with the agent and others play out over a series of radio conversations.

As a player, you've most often seen those conversations play out from the perspective of Big Boss or one of his many clones, but a new game from indie studio 14 Hours Productions asks what if you were instead the person.
A demo of the game will be available as part of Steam Next Fest, which runs from June 15 to 22.
Lovecraft novellas At the Mountains of Madness and The Shadow Out of Time .
Plus, check out the new trailer for the cosmic horror game Penguin Colony.
"Caught between the military's command and your field agent, the orders you're given may conflict with your own morals and interests," says publisher Fellow Traveller.
Each time, you'll have the chance to respond to situations in a few different ways, deciding how best to guide your agent and the amount of information you want to reveal to them and your superiors.
The description continues: "As the pressure mounts, impossible choices will fall on you.
